title: Giving advice
explanation:
Giving advice (A2)

Useful structures:

  • should / shouldn't: You should see a doctor.
  • If I were you, …: If I were you, I would talk to him.
  • Why don't you …? Why don't you take a break?
  • It's a good idea to …

questions.text: [*You should get more sleep.|You should to get more sleep.|You should getting more sleep.]
[*If I were you, I would apologise.|If I am you, I apologise.|If I were you, I will apologise.]
[*Why don't you join a club?|Why you don't join a club?|Why not you join club?]
[*It's a good idea to backup your files.|It's good idea backup files.|Good idea to backuping files.]
questions.feedback: It's a good idea to + verb.

questions.text: You ____ eat so much sugar. | *shouldn't | don't should | not should
You ____ try speaking English every day. | *should | must to | should to
____ you ask your teacher for help? | *Why don't | Why not | What
I ____ talk to your manager if I were you. | *would | will | am
[*You ought to be polite.|You ought being polite.|You ought to being polite.]
[*Have you thought about learning online?|Have you thought learn online?|Thought you learning online?]
[*I don't think you should worry.|I think you shouldn't worrying.|Don't think worry you.]
[*The best thing to do is rest.|Best thing do is rest.|The best is rest thing.]
questions.feedback: The best thing to do is…
